title: 'Nobody can blackmail us': Leaders excoriate Orbán's veto as he tests EU limits
content: The Hungarian leader ups the rhetoric ahead of an April election, pushing the limits of EU rules close to breaking point. António Costa sees his authority challenged in the biggest test yet as European Council president.

title: Ukraine finance minister laments 'bad news from Brussels' as Hungary keeps veto on loan
content: Kyiv says it is doing 'the best we can' after Hungary threw a critical loan into disarray, maintaining a veto on the country over a damaged pipeline.

title: Europe must end diplomacy and push for change in Iran, says Israeli UN ambassador
content: Israel has called for an end to European diplomacy in Iran, urging a firmer hand alongside the US to force regime change in Tehran as the conflict rages on.

title: Energy crisis hits Europe - Who pays the price? MEPs clash in The Ring
content: In this new edition of The Ring, broadcast from the European Parliament in Brussels, MEPs Fabrice Leggeri (Patriots for Europe) and Jussi Saramo (The Left) debate Europe’s energy strategy as geopolitical tensions shake global markets.

title: ‘Iran war is Europe’s war’, says former Trump security advisor
content: Trump’s former national security advisor has slammed European leaders’ inaction on the war in Iran as a “mistake” that invites the US president to halt support for Ukraine against Russia’s full-scale invasion.

title: Special Report: A crunch EU summit torn between two wars and a looming energy crisis
content: As European leaders gather in Brussels for a summit torn between two wars and a looming energy crisis, Euronews presents Special Report, hosted by Maria Tadeo and our team of correspondents. Live tonight at 7 pm Brussels time.

title: Russian gas tanker drifting in the Mediterranean may explode, threatening environmental disaster
content: A Russian ship damaged by explosions has been drifting uncontrollably in the Mediterranean Sea for a fortnight, remaining a potential source of a major environmental accident. Rescuers and environmentalists warn: the situation off the coast of Malta is rapidly becoming more complicated.

title: 'A major mistake', EPP anniversary spoiled by cooperation with far-right in EU Parliament
content: The European People's Party celebrated its 50th anniversary in Brussels amid internal criticism for the alleged cooperation with the far-right in the European Parliament.

title: Europe’s healthcare - A right or a business model? MEPs face off in The Ring
content: In this special edition of The Ring, broadcast from the Euronews Health Summit, MEPs Stine Bosse (Renew Europe) and Tilly Metz (The Greens/EFA) go head-to-head to debate who should pay for European health innovation.

title: Orbán's veto, Iran war and high energy prices set to dominate EU summit
content: Thursday's summit will see EU leaders try to convince Viktor Orbán to lift his controversial veto on the €90 billion to Ukraine. But hopes for a resolution before the Hungarian elections on 12 April are low.

title: Ten EU countries revolt over carbon rules threatening industry ahead of key summit
content: Ahead of a key European summit on Thursday, 10 EU countries intensified attacks on the bloc's climate policy, warning that the current system is damaging industry. They're calling for a slower and more flexible approach to protect competitiveness while pursuing the green transition.

title: European Parliament moves to revive the EU-US trade deal after months of gridlock
content: MEPs moved Tuesday to revive the EU–US trade deal, scheduling a committee vote, but infighting between political groups keeps final approval by the full assembly uncertain with both March and April floated.

title: Ukraine 'informally' opens all six clusters in EU membership bid but veto remains
content: Brussels and Kyiv are trying to keep the process afloat by at least extending the technical talks while the Hungarian veto on Kyiv's membership remains in place.

title: Commission resists overhaul of carbon pricing while pushing for tax cuts on energy
content: Commission resists overhaul of carbon pricing while pushing for tax cuts on energy despite calls from member states to suspend rules as oil prices soar.

title: Is Russia using the war in the Middle East to spread disinformation about Ukraine?
content: Russia has consistently spread disinformation about Ukraine since the start of its full-scale invasion. Now, analysts say the Kremlin is shifting its messaging strategy by exploiting narratives about the war in the Middle East. The Cube debunks some of the false claims.

title: Ukraine accepts EU inspection to Druzhba hoping to lift Hungarian veto on €90bn loan
content: Brussels says Ukraine has accepted an external inspection on the controversial pipeline, even days after President Zelenskyy called it "blackmail". The shift could offer an off-ramp for Hungary to lift its veto.

title: Kallas says 'no appetite' to shift mandate of EU naval mission in the strait of Hormuz
content: The EU's foreign affairs ministers gathered in Brussels a day after US president Donald Trump increased pressure on the bloc to help protect the Strait of Hormuz, warning that NATO faces a “very bad” future if its members fail to come to Washington’s aid.

title: ‘Iran must not be allowed to hold global economy hostage,' UAE minister tells Euronews
content: UAE Minister of State Lana Nusseibeh sent a clear message to EU governments in a Euronews interview, stating that stability in the region is closely tied to Europe’s own economic and security interests.

title: Why is the Strait of Hormuz critical to Europe?
content: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz is affecting energy prices for industries and households, but the vital trade passage also accounts for a significant share of fertiliser shipments, threatening long-term food production. Euronews' explainer goes through the main issues affecting the Strait.

title: EU-US trade remains strong despite tariff pressure, study finds
content: Despite trade tensions, US–EU goods trade hit a record $1.05 trillion in 2025, while the US goods deficit with the bloc fell just 7%, a study by the American Chamber of Commerce to the EU found.

title: Fact check: What do we know about the airstrike on a school in Iran?
content: Conflicting narratives have emerged following the bombing of a school in Iran amid US-Israeli strikes in the country. The Cube breaks down satellite images and news reports that claim to show what happened.

title: Russia pocketing billions from two weeks of war in Iran, data shows
content: Moscow's fossil fuel earnings hit €7.7 billion in the two weeks after joint US-Israeli strikes triggered the war in Iran, as the ongoing conflict has sent global oil prices skyrocketing and led the US to ease Russian sanctions.

title: Slovenia corruption scandal deepens as Prime Minister Robert Golob urges EU probe
content: The scandal has overshadowed the pre-election campaign, leaving the electorate divided and raising tensions across the political spectrum.

title: Belarus releases 250 political prisoners after negotiations with US, envoy says
content: There are more than 1,100 political prisoners in Belarusian jails, many arrested after a disputed 2020 election that triggered widespread protests.

title: Iceland wary of Brexit-like 'lies' ahead of EU referendum, says foreign minister
content: Iceland's foreign minister says now is the time to revive talks to join the EU. Within today's context of instability in transatlantic relations as well as in global politics in general, Iceland must not be left "alone".

title: Watch: European Council meets today. What will be discussed - and why it will be war?
content: Today, 27 EU leaders meet in Brussels for the European Council. The official agenda highlights competitiveness and migration, but war overshadows it all. Your reporter does not yet know exactly what they will say. But we can certainly guess what keeps them up at night.

title: Fact check: Is 'Polexit' really on the cards for Poland?
content: Social media is abuzz with claims that Poles want to leave the EU and that Warsaw is on the cusp of launching the trigger to do so, off the back of a warning by Prime Minister Donald Tusk.

title: MEPs clear path for full adoption of EU–US trade deal
content: EU lawmakers moved closer to implementing the EU–US trade deal after a key committee vote, but tensions with Washington persist.

title: War in Iran: Where in Europe have petrol prices spiked?
content: Motorists across Europe are facing higher fuel prices as the war in Iran has rattled global energy markets, with governments looking at how best to react.

title: Commission failed to meet transparency rules in secret EU-US talks to ease LNG standards
content: The European Commission wants to revise its methane emissions standards after pressure from the US gas industry. At least two key 2025 talks were held privately and not officially registered, raising questions about EU transparency requirements.

title: Newsletter: Iran war and Druzhba dispute to dominate EU leaders’ summit
content: Also in this newsletter: how the 50th anniversary celebration of the centre-right European People's Party (EPP) was overshadowed by allegations of coordinated collaboration with anti-EU forces; and Iceland's Foreign Minister speaks to Europe Today.

title: Europe Today: EU leaders meet in Brussels for a tense, crucial EU summit
content: Tune in to Euronews' new flagship morning programme at 8 am Brussels time. In just 20 minutes, we bring you up to speed on the biggest news of the day.

title: EU summit: Leaders urge 'moratorium' on energy strikes in Middle East
content: Thursday's summit of EU leaders was dominated by Hungary's veto on the €90 billion loan for Ukraine, the spiralling war in the Middle East and the high energy prices that continue to weigh down the economy.

title: ‘48 hours and €100’: EU presents sped-up business registration law
content: The European Commission unveils new rules known as 'the 28th Regime' or 'EU Inc.' to make Europe more attractive for business and better able to compete globally with powers like China and the US.

title: Europe’s far-right unites around Orbán, while Trump’s Hungary visit remains uncertain
content: The event dubbed "the first Patriotic Grand Assembly" will include speeches from far-right leaders Marine Pen and Matteo Salvini as well as Czech Prime Minister Andrej Babiš. According to media reports, US Vice President JD Vance is planning to visit Hungary soon.

title: 'Board of Peace is not an alternative to UN', says UN top humanitarian chief
content: Tom Fletcher defends the UN’s role in a fractured world, insisting new peace initiatives cannot replace the global body.

title: 'Fear Lebanon could be the next Gaza', UN Humanitarian Chief tells Euronews
content: The United Nations' Humanitarian Chief told Euronews it is becoming increasingly dangerous to provide aid in Lebanon. The Israeli operation in the country, which has expanded to include a ground offensive, has killed more than 900 people and displaced more than a million.

title: Netanyahu denies death rumours in social media clip which Grok falsely branded AI
content: Since the US and Israel began their military campaign against Iran on 28 February, Iranian state media and pro-regime accounts have pushed fake news about the death of Netanyahu and other senior Israeli political figures.

title: How resilient are European food systems amid climate change and global conflicts?
content: While global food anchors lead the way, even the most resilient nations are looking vulnerable as the world races to feed a projected 10 billion people by 2050.

title: Von der Leyen to visit Australia as trade deal nears finish line
content: The European Commission is eyeing another deal in its push to diversify trade ties, with President Ursula von der Leyen set to visit Australia from March 23 to 25 to clinch a long-awaited agreement.

title: Is the EU’s Grids Package the answer to energy independence?
content: Energy prices are spiking after the Strait of Hormuz closure, bringing Europe’s vulnerabilities back into focus and turning attention to the EU’s planned Grids Package. Watch the video.

title: Europe Today: Trump blasts NATO allies again as Iran war enters 19th day
content: Tune in to Euronews' new flagship morning programme at 8 am Brussels time. In just 20 minutes, we bring you up to speed on the biggest news of the day.

title: Drug use report: Sharp drop of MDMA in European wastewaters; cocaine and ketamine rise
content: A new study has monitored illicit drug use through wastewater analysis in 115 European cities from 25 countries. From 2024 to 2025, MDMA's residues in wastewaters decreased by 16%, while those of ketamine increased by 41%.

title: 'NATO is not obliged to help Trump in Iran,' Finnish FM Valtonen tells Euronews
content: European NATO allies are rejecting demands by US President Trump to shield the Strait of Hormuz from Iranian disruption. Finnish Foreign Minister Elina Valtonen says while it's not NATO's responsibility to help the US, Europe has "very strong" cards if does decide to send a mission to the region.

title: EU has not ‘legitimised’ Trump’s Board of Peace, Commissioner Šuica says
content: In an interview with Euronews, Commissioner Šuica aimed to distance herself from Trump’s Board of Peace, but did not rule out continuing to attend meetings of the controversial initiative in order to contribute to Gaza’s post-war future.

title: Newsletter: Europe resists US pressure to force re-opening of Strait of Hormuz
content: Also in this newsletter: European Commissioner for the Mediterranean Dubravka Šuica and Finnish Foreign Minister Elena Valtonen speak to Europe Today.

title: How reliant is Spain on trade with the United States?
content: Trump has threatened to cut off trade with Spain over access to its air bases in the Iran war, but how significant is trade between the two countries?

title: Europe Today: EU Commissioner for Mediterranean, Finnish FM speak exclusively to Euronews
content: Tune in to Euronews' new flagship morning programme at 8 am Brussels time. In just 20 minutes, we bring you up to speed on the biggest news of the day.

title: Can the Grids Package free the EU from its energy dependence?
content: US-Iran tensions disrupt Middle East energy routes, driving up oil and gas prices. After the 2022 energy crisis, Brussels launched the Grids Package. Can this plan cut Europe’s reliance on foreign energy?

title: Kallas eyes EU naval mission shift to protect Strait of Hormuz as Trump calls for maritime coalition
content: The EU's 27 foreign ministers meet in Brussels to discuss ways to secure the Strait of Hormuz, as US President Donald Trump urged allies to help open the waterway or face a “very bad” future within NATO.
